compiled_state.title: As a project maintainer, I can validate CI enforcement works via an E2E test
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: An E2E validation test that exercises the full PR lifecycle against a configured repository, verifying that CI checks block and then allow merge.
**Why**: Without an E2E test, enforcement correctness can only be verified manually. This test provides automated confidence that the Ruleset + CI pipeline works end-to-end.
**Scope**:
- IN: Test creates branch, pushes change, creates PR, verifies checks appear and block merge, verifies merge succeeds after checks pass, cleanup of all test resources
- OUT: CI workflow authoring (Story 1), Ruleset configuration (Story 2), onboarding orchestration (Story 4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, an E2E test creates a test branch, pushes a change, creates a PR, verifies CI status checks appear, asserts the PR cannot merge until checks pass, and asserts that a conforming branch merges successfully after checks pass
  ← Satisfies: "An E2E validation test exercises the full PR lifecycle"
- When this story is complete, the E2E test cleans up all created GitHub resources (test branch, test PR) in a finally/cleanup block, even on test failure
  ← Satisfies: "An E2E validation test exercises the full PR lifecycle"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] The E2E test requires a live GitHub repo with admin access and CI enabled. Document environment prerequisites (e.g., GH_TOKEN scope, repo visibility for Rulesets).
- [Reliability] GitHub API rate limits and CI job queue times may cause flaky test results. Consider configurable timeouts and retry logic for polling steps.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. "High confidence" means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.

comments.body: Done definition update (2026-04-26 scope expansion, epic 3aaa-0238 SC8/SC9/SC10): E2E PR-lifecycle test must verify all 11 required checks (was 4 + LLM review = 5; now lint, format, tests, LLM review, validate-ci, linux-bash4, macos-bash3, alpine-busybox, pipefail-strict) appear on the test PR and block merge until green.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Done definition update (adversarial review): E2E test must distinguish 'check passed' from 'check posted at all'. Assertions: (a) each of the 11 named required contexts posts a conclusion (success/failure/skipped) to the test PR — not 'pending' or 'expected'; AND (b) the Ruleset blocks merge when any one of them reports failure (introduce a synthetic failing change to a single check). Without (a), a name-drift typo produces a never-posted phantom check that an E2E only asserting mergeability-after-green would pass.
comments.author: Test
